milestone.title: Conduit — Realtime Voice Conversation + Char Transcription

phase.title: Setup
phase.goal: Python venv, env vars (Deepgram + Groq + ElevenLabs keys), package install (deepgram-sdk groq elevenlabs sounddevice python-dotenv), smoke-test each API individually with 3-line scripts.

phase.title: Part 1 — STT + LLM + TTS conversation loop
phase.goal: Working end-to-end realtime conversation: user speaks → Deepgram transcribes with <300ms latency → Groq LLM responds → ElevenLabs TTS plays audio. All chars (spoken + heard) logged in unified (char, start_time, end_time, notes) store, persisted to JSONL. Requirements: R-001 through R-007.

phase.title: Part 2 — Crosstalk intelligent interruption
phase.goal: Implement tarzain/crosstalk logic: VAD-based interruption detection so LLM begins generating before user finishes speaking; cancel if user continues; minimize cut-offs. Study https://github.com/tarzain/crosstalk before implementing. Requirement: R-008.

phase.title: Ship
phase.goal: GitHub repo created under operator account, RioPopper added as collaborator OR repo made public and URL shared. README written with usage + API key setup instructions.

phase.title: Robustness pass — multi-word LLM trigger, ElevenLabs free-tier, barge-in + echo guard, repo cleanup
phase.goal: Production-readiness fixes after first user testing of the v1 build: ensure the LLM actually fires after a pause, ensure ElevenLabs TTS produces audio on a free-tier account, add user-initiated barge-in on partial words, prevent the bot from interrupting or talking to itself via STT echo, and remove dead runtime scaffolding that reads as junk to outside reviewers.

phase.title: Multi-speaker meeting: 2 bots + 1 human with polite turn-taking
phase.goal: Extend the single-bot conversation pipeline to a simulated 3-speaker meeting with two distinct bot personas (different ELEVENLABS_VOICE_IDs, different system prompts) plus the human. Bots must NOT cut each other off; only humans can barge bots. Turn-taking dispatcher decides who speaks next given the running transcript. Speaker classifier identifies which of the three voices the current mic audio belongs to (extending the existing N-speaker SpeakerClassifier). Each bot's TTS audio flows through the same AEC reference stream. Shared transcript visible to both bot personas. Latency budget: total round-trip per bot turn under 2.5s including LLM + TTS first audio.

phase.title: Real-data evaluation, scenario rotation, operator-voice persistence, GAD evolution
phase.goal: Replace synthetic-only benchmarks with a real ElevenLabs voice corpus, persist operator voice recordings per session, rotate test scenarios (1v1 → 1v2 → 1v3 → repeat) on each setup launch, and run the GAD evolution analysis on phase 04 to surface candidate project skills. Closes the gap between 'we have a benchmark harness' and 'we know our actual numbers on actual voices, including a comparison line to VoxCeleb-O reference scores'. Foundation work for phase 05 (multi-speaker meeting) — the dataset and scenario plumbing land first so the meeting mode lights up cleanly when 05 starts.
